name: Teladoc Health API
what_it_does: Enterprise REST API for embedding Teladoc Health's telehealth platform into employer, health plan, and health system products, enabling virtual video visits, async messaging, and chronic care management.
use_cases: Embedding on-demand virtual urgent care into employer health benefits portals
Integrating telehealth into health plan member apps
Chronic condition management programs (diabetes, hypertension, mental health)
White-label virtual care experience within health system patient portals
Connecting members to specialists via virtual second opinions
not_for: Direct EHR data access — Teladoc manages its own clinical platform
Small practices or individual providers seeking telehealth tools
best_when: Large employer, health plan, or health system needing to embed a proven telehealth network (network of 50,000+ clinicians) into existing member/patient experiences without building telehealth infrastructure from scratch.
avoid_when: You need flexible, developer-first APIs with public documentation and sandbox access, or you're a startup without enterprise partnership resources.

auth.notes: OAuth 2.0 client credentials for server-to-server integration. API keys used for some webhook verification. Credentials provisioned during enterprise partnership onboarding, not via self-service.

pricing.paid_tiers: Per-member-per-month (PMPM) or per-visit pricing negotiated with Teladoc enterprise sales
pricing.notes: No public pricing. Enterprise contracts typically structured as PMPM for health plans or per-visit for employers. Minimum contract sizes apply.

performance.rate_limits: Not publicly documented; governed by enterprise SLA

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: JSON error responses; documentation not publicly available so error patterns are opaque
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Visit scheduling endpoints support idempotency; real-time video session management does not
known_agent_gotchas: No public API documentation — access requires signed enterprise partnership
API is designed for human-initiated virtual visits, not autonomous agent workflows
HIPAA PHI handling means agents must implement strict data handling controls
Integration requires significant onboarding with Teladoc implementation team
Webhook payloads contain PHI, requiring secure endpoint infrastructure
No sandbox or developer trial environment publicly available

security_score_components.security_notes: HIPAA-compliant, HITRUST certified, SOC2 Type II, OAuth 2.0 client credentials, TLS enforced; HIPAA BAA provided as part of enterprise agreement
